claim: Caddy v2.11.2 is the latest stable release; v2.11 was the team's first automated-release-process release; v2.11.2 includes bug fixes plus CVE patches for forward_auth identity injection and vars_regexp placeholder expansion.
quote: Caddy v2.11.2 ... numerous bug fixes and enhancements ... two CVEs related to identity injection in the forward_auth directive and placeholder expansion in vars_regexp.

claim: Caddy 2.11.2 was released 2026-03-05 and is the only supported release per Caddy's support policy.
quote: Caddy 2.11.2 is the currently supported latest stable version, released on March 5, 2026. Only the latest release is considered supported.

claim: HTTP-01 and TLS-ALPN-01 challenges are enabled by default; Caddy picks one at random initially then learns which is most successful; DNS-01 is not on by default because it needs provider credentials.
quote: If multiple challenges are enabled, Caddy chooses one at random to avoid accidental dependence on a particular challenge. Over time, it learns which challenge type is most successful and will begin to prefer it first, but will fall back to other available challenge types if necessary.

claim: DNS-01 is opted into via the tls directive's dns issuer/provider configuration.
quote: tls (Caddyfile directive) — configures TLS, including issuer and DNS challenge providers.

claim: CertMagic is the underlying automatic-HTTPS library that Caddy embeds for ACME issuance and renewal.
quote: Automatic HTTPS for any Go program: fully-managed TLS certificate issuance and renewal.
